flags.concern: Discord resource promotion: late-threshold coverage is still incomplete because the revised rolling 60-day scan misses threads older than 60 days that reach the reaction threshold later.
flags.evidence: v3 commits to concrete single-option decisions for every v2 flag. Discovery drops the 60-day window (anti-join + reaction threshold are sufficient; any qualifying OP imports regardless of age, fixing FLAG-001/correctness-1). Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS` prefilter on member existence plus a parallel `system_logs` insert naming skipped rows, so a single FK violation no longer aborts the sync (fixes FLAG-006/correctness-2). The Cloudflare duration promise is dropped — the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` only writes HLS URL + thumbnail + storage_provider per lines 210-240, and v3's contract matches that exactly (fixes FLAG-005/all_locations-1). The vault bootstrap is replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor migration that applies cleanly on `supabase db reset` without any pre-existing secret; the operator seeds the key via a documented one-line INSERT at provisioning time (fixes all_locations-2). The "existing lightbox" promise for comment media is downgraded to `<a href={media.url} target="_blank">` tiles; a real lightbox is explicitly flagged as follow-up (fixes FLAG-007/scope/callers). The PL/pgSQL pivot from the brief's two-edge-function shape is promoted to an explicit settled decision in the Overview with rationale (addresses issue_hints). Adds a concrete regexp validation step and a missing-member regression test to the Validation Order.

flags.concern: Discord member fallback: the proposed thin upsert is incompatible with the current members schema because username is still required.
flags.evidence: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

flags.concern: Resource typing: importing assets as `tool` conflicts with the website’s existing `lora`/`workflow` assumptions.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan still intentionally diverges from the approved runtime shape, and that remains a flag. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions, a promoter directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable cursor table; v3 still replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. Documenting the deviation is useful, but it does not make it cease to be a deviation from the approved deliverables.
flags.evidence: v5 makes two narrow corrections to v4. (1) Guild ID flows through data rather than a hardcoded frontend constant: a new `discord_guild_id BIGINT` column on `assets` (nullable) and on `asset_comments` (NOT NULL), populated by the promoter from `discord_messages.guild_id`, SELECTed by `useAssetComments` and the four resource hooks, and read directly by `ResourceDetail` when building `https://discord.com/channels/{discord_guild_id}/{discord_thread_id}/{discord_message_id}` jump URLs. This matches the existing multi-server infrastructure (`supabase/migrations/20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145`) and the guild-aware URL helper pattern at `brain-of-bndc/src/common/urls.py:7-12`. Fixes callers / all_locations / FLAG-011. (2) The markdown component reference is redirected from the nonexistent `PostBodyRenderer.tsx` "plugin + sanitization config" to the real pipeline at `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` — `react-markdown` + `remarkGfm` only. Step 18's preferred implementation is for `AssetDescription` to import and wrap `MarkdownRenderer` directly (with inline fallback if reuse is awkward). Drops the "sanitize/rehype" language entirely since no such layer exists in this path. Fixes issue_hints. The PL/pgSQL pivot flag `issue_hints-1` remains gate-settled DEC-003 and is acknowledged once in the Overview per prior guidance.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The revision also drops the requested first-working-version milestone of 'schema + promoter dry-run' before media rehosting. The brief explicitly calls for promoter dry-run behavior, and the repo has multiple existing dry-run patterns in scripts, but v3 has no promoter preview mode at all and jumps straight to live `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources()` validation.
flags.evidence: v4 applies four narrow mechanical corrections to v3 without changing any settled architectural decision. (1) The asset and comment INSERTs now use `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets/asset_comments ...)` plus `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, and drift is handled by a separate conditional UPDATE that only touches rows where `description IS DISTINCT FROM m.content` — so `GET DIAGNOSTICS ROW_COUNT` is genuinely zero on idempotent reruns (fixes FLAG-009/correctness-1). (2) Every `system_logs` INSERT now uses the real column names `logger_name, level, message, extra` per `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` (fixes FLAG-008/correctness-2). (3) A new Phase 1 migration adds the partial composite index `CREATE INDEX discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`, matching the all-history scan's predicates exactly (fixes FLAG-010/all_locations). (4) The promoter function gains a `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` parameter; when TRUE it computes intended-insert counts via SELECT, writes a `level='info'` summary row with `extra->>'dry_run'='true'`, emits `RAISE NOTICE`, and returns without mutating. Execution Order's new first-working-version milestone is `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` before any live run (fixes issue_hints-2). The PL/pgSQL pivot + cursor-removal remains as a gate-endorsed settled decision cited once in the Overview (closes issue_hints-1/issue_hints by acknowledgment — this has been endorsed twice and the plan does not re-open it).

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The main promoter insert still does not implement the anti-join behavior the overview claims. In Step 7, the valid asset insert selects every qualifying OP with an existing member and relies on `ON CONFLICT (discord_thread_id) DO UPDATE`; because there is no `NOT EXISTS` filter on the insert path, every rerun will re-update already imported assets and `GET DIAGNOSTICS v_a = ROW_COUNT` will not stay at zero, which conflicts with the plan’s own idempotency/counting claims.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The observability SQL in the plan is still mismatched to the actual schema. `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` defines `system_logs` with `logger_name` and `extra` columns, not `component` and `payload`, yet v3 still proposes inserts like `INSERT INTO public.system_logs (component, level, message, payload) ...`. As written, those logging statements would fail until the column names are corrected.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The plan scopes the frontend work around `ResourceModal`, but `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` only finds the component definition and no callers. The live resource surface is `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which still renders `resource.description` as plain text at lines 166-169 and already owns the visible 'Made with this' gallery. Limiting the change to the dead modal leaves the actual resource page behavior unchanged.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.

flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The Cloudflare reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository code. `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` only writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`; it does not persist duration, even though the revised plan and success criteria say the existing webhook will populate HLS, thumbnail, and duration for imported videos. That supporting-infrastructure contract is still inaccurate unless the plan adds a webhook change or drops duration from the promise.

flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The new vault bootstrap step is not yet self-contained enough to satisfy the plan’s own `supabase db reset` criterion. There is no existing `vault.create_secret`, `internal.get_service_role_key`, or secret-seeding pattern anywhere under `supabase/`, and the proposed migration depends on an out-of-band `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key_value', true)` value that the repo never sets. As written, the bootstrap is still an infrastructure gap rather than a ready migration recipe.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I traced the current callers that consume resource rows, and they all pass through the existing hooks with narrow assumptions about the payload: `useResources.ts` reads `id, type, name, description, admin_status, ...`, `useCommunityResources.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, lora_link, ...`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, ...` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`). None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I also checked the direct callers of the UI surface the plan wants to edit. `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` returns only the component definition and no imports/usages, which means there are currently no callers passing assets into that modal at all. The meaningful caller path today is the route-driven `ResourceDetail` page, so the proposed fix is landing at the wrong boundary unless the plan also restores or introduces actual `ResourceModal` usage.
flags.evidence: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

flags.concern: Discord promoter execution: the revised SQL-native promoter still lacks row-level error isolation, so one missing-member FK or other bad row can abort the whole sync instead of being logged and skipped.
flags.evidence: v3 commits to concrete single-option decisions for every v2 flag. Discovery drops the 60-day window (anti-join + reaction threshold are sufficient; any qualifying OP imports regardless of age, fixing FLAG-001/correctness-1). Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS` prefilter on member existence plus a parallel `system_logs` insert naming skipped rows, so a single FK violation no longer aborts the sync (fixes FLAG-006/correctness-2). The Cloudflare duration promise is dropped — the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` only writes HLS URL + thumbnail + storage_provider per lines 210-240, and v3's contract matches that exactly (fixes FLAG-005/all_locations-1). The vault bootstrap is replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor migration that applies cleanly on `supabase db reset` without any pre-existing secret; the operator seeds the key via a documented one-line INSERT at provisioning time (fixes all_locations-2). The "existing lightbox" promise for comment media is downgraded to `<a href={media.url} target="_blank">` tiles; a real lightbox is explicitly flagged as follow-up (fixes FLAG-007/scope/callers). The PL/pgSQL pivot from the brief's two-edge-function shape is promoted to an explicit settled decision in the Overview with rationale (addresses issue_hints). Adds a concrete regexp validation step and a missing-member regression test to the Validation Order.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: There is still a smaller repo mismatch in the markdown guidance. v4 says `AssetDescription` should mirror `PostBodyRenderer.tsx`'s 'plugin + sanitization config', but `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/PostBodyRenderer.tsx:22-50` just hands text segments to `MarkdownTextSegment`, and `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` uses `react-markdown` with `remarkGfm` only; there is no separate sanitize/rehype config in this path today. The implementation note should point to the real `MarkdownRenderer` reuse path instead of a nonexistent sanitization layer.
flags.evidence: v5 makes two narrow corrections to v4. (1) Guild ID flows through data rather than a hardcoded frontend constant: a new `discord_guild_id BIGINT` column on `assets` (nullable) and on `asset_comments` (NOT NULL), populated by the promoter from `discord_messages.guild_id`, SELECTed by `useAssetComments` and the four resource hooks, and read directly by `ResourceDetail` when building `https://discord.com/channels/{discord_guild_id}/{discord_thread_id}/{discord_message_id}` jump URLs. This matches the existing multi-server infrastructure (`supabase/migrations/20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145`) and the guild-aware URL helper pattern at `brain-of-bndc/src/common/urls.py:7-12`. Fixes callers / all_locations / FLAG-011. (2) The markdown component reference is redirected from the nonexistent `PostBodyRenderer.tsx` "plugin + sanitization config" to the real pipeline at `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` — `react-markdown` + `remarkGfm` only. Step 18's preferred implementation is for `AssetDescription` to import and wrap `MarkdownRenderer` directly (with inline fallback if reuse is awkward). Drops the "sanitize/rehype" language entirely since no such layer exists in this path. Fixes issue_hints. The PL/pgSQL pivot flag `issue_hints-1` remains gate-settled DEC-003 and is acknowledged once in the Overview per prior guidance.

flags.concern: Discord sync logging: the revised plan still targets nonexistent `system_logs` columns, so the proposed promoter/importer logging SQL would fail against the actual schema.
flags.evidence: v4 applies four narrow mechanical corrections to v3 without changing any settled architectural decision. (1) The asset and comment INSERTs now use `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets/asset_comments ...)` plus `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, and drift is handled by a separate conditional UPDATE that only touches rows where `description IS DISTINCT FROM m.content` — so `GET DIAGNOSTICS ROW_COUNT` is genuinely zero on idempotent reruns (fixes FLAG-009/correctness-1). (2) Every `system_logs` INSERT now uses the real column names `logger_name, level, message, extra` per `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` (fixes FLAG-008/correctness-2). (3) A new Phase 1 migration adds the partial composite index `CREATE INDEX discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`, matching the all-history scan's predicates exactly (fixes FLAG-010/all_locations). (4) The promoter function gains a `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` parameter; when TRUE it computes intended-insert counts via SELECT, writes a `level='info'` summary row with `extra->>'dry_run'='true'`, emits `RAISE NOTICE`, and returns without mutating. Execution Order's new first-working-version milestone is `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` before any live run (fixes issue_hints-2). The PL/pgSQL pivot + cursor-removal remains as a gate-endorsed settled decision cited once in the Overview (closes issue_hints-1/issue_hints by acknowledgment — this has been endorsed twice and the plan does not re-open it).

flags.concern: Discord promoter idempotency: the revised main insert path still lacks the claimed anti-join, so reruns will update every already-imported qualifying OP and its row-count/idempotency claims will not hold as written.

That is a brittle supporting-infrastructure mismatch rather than a necessity imposed by the repository.

flags.concern: Discord guild propagation: v5 assumes `discord_messages.guild_id` is always populated, but the repository does not enforce that invariant before copying it into the new `asset_comments.discord_guild_id BIGINT NOT NULL` column.
flags.evidence: `supabase/migrations/20260320000000_multi_server.sql:115-117` only adds `guild_id` to `discord_messages`, and `supabase/migrations/20260320160000_enforce_guild_constraints.sql:12-17` backfills some nulls, but there is no `ALTER TABLE discord_messages ALTER COLUMN guild_id SET NOT NULL` anywhere in the repo.

flags.concern: Criterion 40: requires human verification (subjective_judgment).

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The new `discord_guild_id` propagation is not fully supported by the actual schema guarantees. `supabase/migrations/20260320000000_multi_server.sql:115-117` only adds `guild_id` to `discord_messages`, and `20260320160000_enforce_guild_constraints.sql:12-17` backfills some nulls from `discord_channels`, but the repository never sets `discord_messages.guild_id` to `NOT NULL`. V5 then makes `asset_comments.discord_guild_id BIGINT NOT NULL` and says the promoter copies `m.guild_id` directly, so historical messages with a still-null guild_id would either fail inserts or need an explicit skip/log path that the plan does not yet spell out.
